name: Herbie Hancock
abstract: Herbert Jeffrey Hancock (born April 12, 1940) is an American jazz musician, bandleader, and composer. He started his career with trumpeter Donald Byrd's group.

interviews.title: Herbie Hancock interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, November 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: A couple with Herbie Hancock — “Maiden Voyage” and “Speak Like a Child.” ... We rehearsed it two days at a rehearsal hall and went to the studio and made the record in one day.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 17, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Denny Zeitlin
about_quotes.doc_title: Denny Zeitlin interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: I haven’t had the pleasure of playing with Herbie Hancock yet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: That’s how I met Herbie Hancock... And we rehearsed. We go to the restaurant in Long Island... We talked yesterday about Herbie Hancock. There was a recording you did named Toots in New York in 1968 with Herbie on the date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: I remember that the next LP was of a guy almost balding... And I find I enjoy that, and then that happen to me again with the Miles Davis new quintet, the quintet with Herbie Hancock and Tony Williams and Ron Carter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: in walks Herbie Hancock you know, on the piano... That's his first time out... I still think that’s one of the best recordings of Jackie and all of us on there.
